question: What data do you collect? What do you do with it? How do you ensure data is only used for stated purposes?
title: Data Minimization, Purpose Limitation, and Usage Governance
bullets: Enumerate all collected data elements (content, metadata, telemetry, identifiers) with clear definitions
Map each data element to a specific, justified purpose and legal basis where applicable
Describe controls enforcing purpose limitation (tagging, RBAC/ABAC, policy engines, audit trails)
Explain processes preventing function creep (reviews, approvals, monitoring, contractual restrictions)
suggested_inputs: Data inventory/register with fields, purposes, storage locations, and retention
Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) or privacy impact documentation
Access control policies (RBAC/ABAC) and data classification/tagging schema
Audit logs demonstrating purpose-based access and usage monitoring

good.text: We maintain a detailed data inventory listing all elements (user inputs, metadata, logs, identifiers). Each item is mapped to a specific purpose and retention period. We explicitly list data we do NOT collect and justify necessity for each collected element.

Purpose limitation is enforced via data tagging/classification, RBAC/ABAC policies, and policy engines that restrict access by purpose. Organizational controls (training, audits) and contractual terms (DPA) prohibit secondary use without consent.
We conduct quarterly data minimization audits. We regularly remove unnecessary fields and reduce retention where possible. Changes are documented with impact assessments and approvals.

We prevent function creep through change management controls, purpose-based access logs, anomaly detection for unusual data usage, and approval workflows for any new data use. All usage is auditable against declared purposes.

partial.text: We collect certain user data and logs necessary for service operation, but a complete, auditable inventory with purpose mapping is not consistently maintained.
Data is used only for intended purposes, though enforcement relies primarily on internal policies rather than technical controls.
We review data collection periodically, but there is no formal cadence or documented minimization audit process.
Data may be used to improve services, but controls around secondary use and third-party sharing are not clearly defined.

bad.text: We collect only what is necessary without specifying data elements or purposes.
We follow standard data collection practices without detailing what is collected or why.
We may use customer data to improve our services without clearly defining scope, consent, or controls.
We are unable to provide a complete list of data elements we collect or how they are used.

follow_ups: Provide a complete data inventory listing each data element, purpose, storage location, and retention period.
How do you technically enforce purpose limitation and prevent use beyond consented purposes?
Do you share or sell data to third parties? If yes, list entities, purposes, and controls.
Show audit evidence that data access aligns with declared purposes.
What governance prevents function creep when introducing new features or analytics?
